h2h_summary: Australia have the marginal edge in the only two meetings between these sides — both friendlies — winning one and drawing one, with Paraguay yet to record a victory.
wc_history_home: Paraguay have appeared at nine World Cups, reaching their best finish of the quarter-finals in 2010 before losing 1-0 to eventual champions Spain; this 2026 tournament marks their return after a 16-year absence.
wc_history_away: Australia have appeared at six World Cups (1974, 2006–2026), with their best results being two Round of 16 appearances — losing narrowly to eventual champions Italy in 2006 and to eventual champions Argentina in 2022.
group_context: Group D contains co-hosts USA (tournament favourites), Türkiye, Paraguay, and Australia; the Paraguay vs Australia match on June 25 is the final group-stage fixture for both sides and could be decisive for second and third place, with the top two advancing automatically and the best third-placed teams also progressing.
strengths_home: Exceptional defensive discipline — conceded only 10 goals in qualifying, fewest in CONMEBOL
Dangerous transition game through Miguel Almiron's pace and directness
Strong set-piece organisation and compact midfield press
strengths_away: Disciplined back-three defensive structure with Harry Souttar's aerial dominance
Explosive attacking pace through Nestory Irankunda and Martin Boyle on the flanks
High work rate, pressing intensity and set-piece threat throughout the squad
team_style_home: Paraguay under Gustavo Alfaro deploy a disciplined 4-4-2 or 4-2-3-1 built on defensive solidity and collective pressing intensity, relying on structured low blocks and rapid counter-attacks through Almiron's transition quality rather than sustained possession.
team_style_away: Australia under Tony Popovic use a back-three system (3-4-2-1 / 3-4-3) with energetic wing-backs, compact defensive organisation, high pressing intensity, and quick direct counter-attacking football rather than dominant possession.
context: Group D contains co-hosts USA (tournament favourites), Türkiye, Paraguay, and Australia; the Paraguay vs Australia match on June 25 is the final group-stage fixture for both sides and could be decisive for second and third place, with the top two advancing automatically and the best third-placed teams also progressing.
